Key takeaways
→
espazium.ch
Renovation and reuse dominate the editorial audience across languages: 2 of the top 5 DE articles are conversions (Domino Riehen, Globus Basel) and 4 of the 5 FR articles are transformation projects in the «Réalisation» category. The EPFL kitchen research piece achieves the best DE engagement time (1'44''). SEO remains the main driver with 69% of traffic from organic search.
→
competitions
School competitions dominate the ranking: 3 of the 5 most visited competitions are schools (Viererfeld 1238, Wolfacker 1094, Ochsenmatt 911 users). With 2 min 01 s average engagement and 39% direct traffic, competitions.espazium.ch confirms a loyal professional audience that returns on its own.
→
Multimedia & Social
Cross-promotion of competitions performs best on social media: the «Currently on competitions.espazium.ch» posts rank #1 on Instagram (15,860 views, 448 interactions) and top post on LinkedIn. The Foundation Award 2026 posts drove strong engagement at the end of the month. Newsletters remain stable at 25.4% open rate and 9.6% click rate, peaking at 13.2% clicks for Newsletter-DE #13 (Globus Basel).

Key Takeaway & Comment — Social Media & Newsletters

Glossary

Key metrics explained
Google Analytics 4 (GA4)
Active Users
GA4
The number of distinct people who visited the site and had at least one meaningful interaction (page view, scroll, click, etc.) during the selected period. This is the most reliable measure of actual audience reach.
e.g. 12,450 active users in April = 12,450 different people visited the site.
Page Views
GA4
The total number of times any page was loaded or reloaded. One user can generate multiple page views in a single visit. Useful for measuring content exposure, but can be inflated by the same person refreshing a page.
e.g. 38,200 page views from 12,450 users = avg. ~3 pages per person.
Avg. Engagement Time
GA4
The average time the browser was in focus and the user was actively interacting with the page. Unlike older "session duration", this metric ignores inactive tabs — making it a more honest measure of real reading time.
e.g. 1'48" avg. engagement = readers spend about 2 minutes actively reading.
Sessions
GA4
A group of interactions from one user within a defined time window. One person can have multiple sessions (e.g. visiting in the morning and again in the evening). Less precise than Active Users for measuring reach.
e.g. 15,000 sessions from 12,450 users = some people visited more than once.
Organic Search
GA4 — Traffic source
Visitors who arrived via a search engine (Google, Bing, etc.) by clicking on a non-paid result. This is the most sustainable traffic source and reflects SEO performance.
e.g. Searching "prix SIA 2026" and clicking the espazium result.
Direct
GA4 — Traffic source
Visitors who typed the URL directly into their browser, used a bookmark, or arrived from a source GA4 couldn't identify (e.g. some email clients, messaging apps). Often includes loyal, returning readers.
e.g. Typing "espazium.ch" directly in the browser.
Referral
GA4 — Traffic source
Visitors who clicked a link on another website (not a search engine or social network). This includes press mentions, partner sites, Wikipedia links, etc. A good indicator of editorial influence.
e.g. A link to an article from archINFORM.net or SIA.ch.
Organic Social
GA4 — Traffic source
Visitors who clicked a link shared on a social media platform (Instagram, LinkedIn, Facebook, etc.) without paid promotion. Reflects the organic reach of social media posts.
e.g. A follower clicking a link in an Instagram post.
Unassigned
GA4 — Traffic source
Traffic that GA4 could not categorize into any known channel. Often caused by missing UTM parameters, non-standard referrers, or certain privacy browsers. A high unassigned rate may indicate UTM tagging issues.
e.g. Clicks from a newsletter without UTM tags may land here.
UTM Parameters
GA4 / Mailchimp
Short tracking codes added to URLs to tell GA4 where a visitor came from. Essential for tracking newsletter traffic accurately. Without them, newsletter clicks appear as "Direct" or "Unassigned".
e.g. ?utm_source=mailchimp&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=april-nl-de
Mailchimp — Newsletters
Open Rate
Mailchimp
The percentage of recipients who opened the email. Calculated as: (unique opens ÷ delivered emails) × 100. A good benchmark for architecture/culture newsletters is 30–45%. Below 20% warrants attention.
e.g. 38% open rate = 38 out of 100 recipients opened the newsletter.
Click Rate
Mailchimp
The percentage of recipients who clicked at least one link in the email. A stronger signal of engagement than open rate, as it requires active intent. Industry average for media newsletters is 2–5%.
e.g. 4.2% click rate = ~4 out of 100 readers clicked a link.
Click Map
Mailchimp
A visual overlay showing which links in a newsletter were clicked and how often. Helps identify which content, position, or CTA drives the most engagement. Available in Mailchimp campaign reports.
e.g. The main article image gets 3× more clicks than the text link below it.
Unsubscribes
Mailchimp
The number of people who opted out of the mailing list after receiving a campaign. A rate above 0.5% per send is a warning sign. Monitor spikes — they often correlate with content relevance or send frequency.
e.g. 3 unsubscribes out of 800 recipients = 0.37% — within normal range.
Podigee — Podcast
Listens / Downloads
Podigee
The number of times an episode was streamed or downloaded. Podigee counts a listen only after a minimum threshold of playback (typically 60 seconds), filtering out accidental plays.
e.g. 420 listens = 420 people (or plays) that went past the threshold.
Completion Rate
Podigee
The percentage of the episode that listeners heard on average. A high completion rate (above 70%) indicates the content held attention throughout. Low rates may signal the episode is too long or loses listeners early.
e.g. 74% completion on a 25-minute episode = listeners heard ~18.5 minutes.
Subscribers
Podigee
People who have subscribed to the podcast feed via an app (Spotify, Apple Podcasts, etc.) and automatically receive new episodes. A stable subscriber base is a stronger signal than raw listen counts.
e.g. 820 subscribers = 820 people who opted in to receive every episode.
Avg. Listen Duration
Podigee
The average length of time listeners spent with an episode. Complements completion rate — useful when episodes vary significantly in length across the month.
e.g. 18'42" avg. duration on a 25-minute episode = 74% completion rate.
Social Media — Instagram & LinkedIn
Reach
Instagram / LinkedIn
The number of unique accounts that saw a post at least once. Unlike impressions, reach counts each person only once regardless of how many times they saw the content. The best measure of actual audience exposure.
e.g. Reach of 3,200 = 3,200 different people saw the post.
Impressions
Instagram / LinkedIn
The total number of times content was displayed, including multiple views by the same person. Always higher than reach. Useful for measuring overall content visibility and frequency of exposure.
e.g. 5,800 impressions from 3,200 reach = avg. 1.8 views per person.
Engagement Rate
Instagram / LinkedIn
The percentage of people who interacted with a post (likes, comments, shares, saves, clicks) relative to reach or followers. A key indicator of content quality. Good benchmarks: Instagram 2–5%, LinkedIn 1–3%.
e.g. 148 interactions ÷ 3,200 reach = 4.6% engagement rate.
Followers / Subscribers
Instagram / LinkedIn
The total number of accounts following the page. Growth in followers reflects brand awareness over time. Monthly change (±) is more meaningful than the absolute number for tracking momentum.
e.g. +42 followers in April = steady organic growth without paid promotion.
espazium.ch — Digital Lab Report edited on 02 July 2026

Copy current values to M-1 fields ── setTimeout(() => { CALC_IDS.forEach(id => { const cur = document.getElementById(id); const prev = document.getElementById(id+'p'); if(cur && prev) prev.value = cur.value; }); // RS fields M-1 RS_IDS.forEach(id => { const cur = document.getElementById(id); const prev = document.getElementById(id+'p'); if(cur && prev) prev.value = cur.value; }); // ── 5. Advance period ── p.value = m===12 ? `${y+1}-01` : `${y}-${String(m+1).padStart(2,'0')}`; // ── 6. Clear current month fields (keep M-1) ── allIds().forEach(id => { // Skip M-1 fields and period if(id.endsWith('p') || id === 'period') return; const e = document.getElementById(id); if(!e) return; if(e.tagName === 'SELECT') e.selectedIndex = 0; else e.value = ''; }); recalcAll(); updLang(); updSrc1(); updSrc2(); buildSummary(); save(); alert('✓ Month closed!\n\n→ espazium-report-' + slug + '.html downloaded — upload to Netlify Drop\n→ espazium-report-' + slug + '.json downloaded — keep as backup\n\nThe report is now ready for ' + months[m===12?0:m] + ' ' + (m===12?y+1:y) + '.'); }, 800); } // ── EXPORT / IMPORT ── function exportHTML() { var d = {}; document.querySelectorAll('[id]').forEach(function(el) { if((el.tagName==='INPUT'||el.tagName==='TEXTAREA'||el.tagName==='SELECT') && el.id) { d[el.id] = el.value; } }); var period = document.getElementById('period')?.value || 'export'; var dataJson = JSON.stringify(d); var ot = '<'+'script>'; var ct = '<'+'/script>'; var lines = [ ot, 'window.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded",function(){', // Populate all fields 'var data='+dataJson+';', 'Object.keys(data).forEach(function(id){var el=document.getElementById(id);if(el)el.value=data[id];});', // Reset all tabs and sections to initial state 'document.querySelectorAll(".sec").forEach(function(s){s.classList.remove("on");});', 'document.querySelectorAll(".tab").forEach(function(t){t.classList.remove("on");});', 'var s0=document.getElementById("sec0");if(s0)s0.classList.add("on");', 'var t0=document.querySelector(".tab[data-n=\'0\']");if(t0)t0.classList.add("on");', // Run all update functions 'setTimeout(function(){', 'if(typeof updLang==="function")updLang();', 'if(typeof updSrc1==="function")updSrc1();', 'if(typeof updSrc2==="function")updSrc2();', 'if(typeof recalcAll==="function")recalcAll();', 'if(typeof restoreAllLinks==="function")restoreAllLinks();', 'if(typeof updNlSummary==="function")updNlSummary();', 'if(typeof buildSummary==="function")buildSummary();', 'var t2=document.getElementById("tipTitle2");', 'if(t2&&t2.value){var c2=document.getElementById("tip-card-2");var b2=document.getElementById("tipAddBtn");if(c2)c2.style.display="block";if(b2)b2.style.display="none";}', 'try{localStorage.setItem("esp_v5",JSON.stringify(data));}catch(e){}', '},300);});', ct ]; var injectScript = lines.join('\n'); // Get the clean source HTML — use the original page source, not outerHTML var source = '\n' + document.documentElement.outerHTML; // Remove any previously injected auto-load scripts source = source.replace(/\n?
